topic: avifenesh/glide-mq - message queue library built on Valkey GLIDE

sources.title: glide-mq GitHub Repository
keyInsights: High-performance Node.js message queue with Rust NAPI core
1-RTT-per-job optimization via Valkey Functions (FCALL)
7 AI-native primitives for LLM orchestration
Apache 2.0 license, v0.15.1
Hash-tagged keys for zero-config clustering

sources.title: glide-mq README
keyInsights: Complete feature matrix with categories
Performance benchmarks on AWS ElastiCache Valkey 8.2
35-38% throughput gain over BullMQ at c=10-15
Ecosystem packages: dashboard, framework integrations
When NOT to use glide-mq (Kafka-style streaming, browsers, exactly-once)

sources.title: glide-mq Usage Guide
keyInsights: Queue class: add, addBulk (12.7x faster), getJob, getJobs, getJobCounts, getMetrics
Worker events: active, completed, failed, error, stalled, drained
Job options: delay, priority, attempts, backoff, timeout, ttl, lifo, ordering
LIFO uses separate Valkey LIST (RPUSH/RPOP)
Metrics recorded server-side inside Valkey Functions with zero extra RTTs

sources.title: glide-mq Workflow Patterns
keyInsights: FlowProducer creates atomic job trees (parents wait for children)
addDAG() supports arbitrary DAG topologies with deps arrays
chain() for sequential, group() for parallel, chord() for parallel+callback
Dynamic children via moveToWaitingChildren()
Cycle detection throws CycleError

sources.title: glide-mq Advanced Features
keyInsights: Cron schedulers with timezone, fixed intervals, bounded runs
Dual-axis rate limiting: RPM + TPM with per-group sliding windows
Pluggable serializers and transparent gzip compression
Shared client option reduces connections from N+2 to 2
Job revocation via cooperative cancellation

sources.title: glide-mq Internal Architecture
keyInsights: Key layout: glide:{queueName}:* with hash-tagged clustering
44 named Valkey Functions in glidemq library (vs 53 EVAL scripts in BullMQ)
Priority encoding: score = priority * 2^42 + timestamp_ms
Consumer group per queue, XAUTOCLAIM for stall recovery
Connection model: separate commandClient and blockingClient per worker

sources.title: BullMQ to glide-mq Migration Guide
keyInsights: Connection format change: {host,port} to {addresses:[{host,port}]}
group.id replaced by ordering.key (free vs Pro license)
Many APIs identical: backoff, attemptsMade, updateData, limiter
Rust NAPI client replaces ioredis JavaScript client
1 persistent function library replaces 53 Lua scripts

sources.title: glide-mq Observability Guide
keyInsights: Auto OpenTelemetry spans with no code changes required
Per-minute metrics with 24-hour retention
AI usage telemetry: reportUsage, getFlowUsage, getUsageSummary
SSE surfaces for real-time event and stream monitoring
Telemetry can be disabled for max throughput

sources.title: glide-mq Testing Guide
keyInsights: TestQueue and TestWorker for in-memory testing without Valkey
Supports retries, batch mode, custom job IDs
searchJobs() for filtering by state and data fields
Works with Vitest/Jest
Mirrors production behavior including deduplication

sources.title: glide-mq Broadcast/Pub-Sub Guide
keyInsights: Fan-out to all subscribers (vs point-to-point queue)
NATS-style subject filtering with * and > wildcards
BroadcastWorker with subscription names and subject arrays
startFrom controls replay ($ for new, 0-0 for history)
HTTP SSE exposure for external consumers

sources.title: glide-mq Serverless Deployment Guide
keyInsights: Producer class returns string IDs with minimal overhead
ServerlessPool manages connection reuse across warm Lambda invocations
HTTP proxy for Edge/Cloudflare Workers without NAPI
addBulk pipelines jobs in single round trip
All Queue.add() features available through Producer

sources.title: glide-mq Step Jobs Guide
keyInsights: moveToDelayed(timestamp, nextStep) pauses and sets next step
State persists through step transitions in job.data.step
Eliminates need for separate queues in multi-step workflows
Combines with moveToWaitingChildren for orchestration
Drip campaign example demonstrates practical usage

sources.title: glide-mq Cross-Language Wire Protocol
keyInsights: Non-Node languages use standard FCALL commands
glidemq_addJob takes 4 keys and 21 args
Job hash has 30+ fields including AI usage metrics
Compression format: gz: + base64(gzip(data)), 1MB max
Custom job ID validation: max 256 chars, no {}: characters

sources.title: glide-mq Durability Guarantees
keyInsights: At-least-once delivery semantics
Stalled jobs recovered from PEL via XAUTOCLAIM
AOF appendfsync everysec recommended minimum
Async replication means primary failure can lose recent writes
Processors must be idempotent

sources.title: glide-mq Valkey Functions Source
keyInsights: 44 named functions in glidemq library (version 81)
Atomic completeAndFetchNext eliminates wasted HMGET calls
Token bucket with remainder accumulator for precision
Skip markers handle debounce deletion without sequence gaps
Separate stall detection for LIFO/priority list-sourced jobs

sources.title: glide-mq Changelog
keyInsights: Rapid release cadence: 0.8.0 to 0.15.1 in ~6 weeks
Breaking changes in 0.12.0 (group key type) and 0.14.0 (usage API)
v0.10.0 achieved 108% throughput gain at c=1
v0.13.0 introduced suspend/resume and vector search
v0.15.0 added HTTP proxy and Flow API

sources.title: glide-mq package.json
keyInsights: Version 0.15.1, Node.js >=20
Single runtime dependency: @glidemq/speedkey ^0.3.0
Exports: main, testing, proxy modules
Dev deps include BullMQ and ioredis for benchmarking
Build: TypeScript compilation, Vitest for testing

sources.title: glide-mq Documentation Site
keyInsights: Comprehensive doc site with guide, API reference, examples
Integration guides for Hono, Fastify, NestJS, Hapi
Migration guides from BullMQ and Bee-Queue
Example categories: basics, scheduling, workflows, AI pipelines
Requires Valkey 7.0+ (vector search needs valkey-bundle)

sources.title: Valkey GLIDE - Official Client Library
keyInsights: Rust-first client with language bindings (Java, Python, Node, Go)
Cluster-aware operations, AZ affinity routing
Backed by AWS and GCP
glide-mq uses the Node.js NAPI binding via @glidemq/speedkey
Eliminates JavaScript protocol parsing overhead

sources.title: Valkey Streams Introduction
keyInsights: Append-only log with O(1) random access
Consumer groups for server-side load balancing
PEL tracks unacknowledged messages per consumer
XAUTOCLAIM automates recovery from unresponsive consumers
Time-based IDs enable free range queries

sources.title: Valkey Functions Introduction
keyInsights: Persistent across restarts (stored in RDB/AOF)
Named functions vs SHA1 digests of EVAL
Functions can call other functions within same library
Atomic library replacement via FUNCTION LOAD REPLACE
Cluster deployment: load on all primaries

sources.title: Avi Fenesh - glide-mq Creator Profile
keyInsights: System Software Engineer at AWS ElastiCache
Member of AWS, Amazon Contributing, and Valkey-io organizations
Maintains both Valkey GLIDE client and glide-mq
Expertise in Rust, C, Node.js
Also develops agent-sh ecosystem (Agnix, AgentSys)
